name: Master Spec — Full Multi-Phase Build
description: Implements all 6 Master Spec phases using module-pattern (parallel create → serial wire). Phase 0 (permission UX) and Phase 4.4 (mechanics) are complete — this DAG covers the remaining phases.

nodes.description: Phase 1: /compact — Context Compaction Engine (token velocity, state condensation, artifact protection)
nodes.prompt_template: Create scripts/compact-module.py with MCP tools: read_compact_token_velocity, read_compact_synthesize, read_compact_status, write_compact_execute, read_compact_record_tick

nodes.description: Phase 2: /mutate — Algorithmic Mutation Engine (scope assessment, redundancy audit, execution)
nodes.prompt_template: Create scripts/mutation-module.py with MCP tools: read_mutate_scope, read_mutate_audit, mutate_execute, read_mutate_status

nodes.description: Phase 3: Plumber Module (socket inspection, handoff tracing, artifact checkpointing)
nodes.prompt_template: Create scripts/plumber-module.py with MCP tools: read_plumber_inspect_socket, read_plumber_trace_handoff, write_plumber_checkpoint, read_plumber_checkpoints

nodes.description: Phase 4.1: Math Engine (matrix solve, ODE solver, ASCII plot, LaTeX generator)
nodes.prompt_template: Create scripts/sim-math-module.py with MCP tools: read_sim_matrix_solve, read_sim_ode, read_sim_latex, read_sim_plot

nodes.description: Phase 5: Pedagogy Engine (assess understanding, adapt depth, store profile)
nodes.prompt_template: Create scripts/pedagogy-module.py with MCP tools: read_pedagogy_assess, read_pedagogy_adapt, write_pedagogy_profile

nodes.description: Phase 5: Consolidation Daemon (offline cross-pollination, TF-IDF similarity linking)
nodes.prompt_template: Create scripts/consolidation-daemon.py with MCP tools: read_consolidation_status, mutate_consolidation_run, read_consolidation_links

nodes.description: Wire all new tools into tools-mcp-server.py: add tool definitions to TOOLS list, add dispatch handlers to handle_tool_call
nodes.prompt_template: Add tool definitions and dispatch handlers to tools-mcp-server.py for all newly created module files. Each module needs: 1) TOOLS list entries with permission annotations, 2) dispatch block in handle_tool_call(), 3) proper JSON-RPC error handling.

nodes.description: Run cross-phase verification and tool count check
nodes.prompt_template: Run full verification: python scripts/phase-verify-full.py && python scripts/tools-mcp-server.py --list-tools | python -c "import sys,json; t=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); print(f'{len(t)} tools registered')"

merge_rules.strategy: All module files are independent — merge order does not matter. Each module is its own .py file.
Wiring edits tools-mcp-server.py. Must be serial — no parallel access allowed.
